message:
style: overhaul design system — tighter palette, Inter headings, minimal component vocabulary

Simplify and tighten the visual design language across the entire article:

CSS Custom Properties:
- Remove redundant accent variables (--accent-blue, --accent-purple, --accent-emerald,
  --glass-bg, --glass-border); keep only --accent-cyan as the primary interactive color
- Add typographic scale variables (--fs-base: 1.1rem, --lh-base: 1.6) for consistent
  body text sizing across all sections
- Shift --text-muted from #94a3b8 to #64748b (darker, less competing with body text)

Typography:
- Switch headings from Bricolage Grotesque to Inter at font-weight 900 with -0.05em
  tracking; this creates a heavier, more editorial feel that contrasts better with
  the light body text
- Add OpenType features ss03, cv05, tnum for improved numeral alignment in the
  math-heavy context of the article
- Cap paragraph width at 65ch and enable text-wrap: pretty for better line breaks;
  reduce paragraph margin from mb-8 to mb-6 for tighter vertical rhythm

Component Classes:
- Reduce border-radius across all components (insight-card 2.5rem→2xl, viz-container
  3rem→3xl) for a cleaner, less bubbly feel
- Remove insight-card ::after pseudo-element (was just a duplicate border)
- Simplify section-divider from a gradient to a flat white/10 line with reduced
  vertical margin (my-32→my-16)
- Reduce viz-container min-height and glow shadow for lighter visual weight
- Tighten button sizing (btn-action, btn-secondary) and use consistent 10px/0.2em
  tracking; switch btn-action from white to cyan-500 background
- Add new utility classes: .drop-cap (replaces verbose inline first-letter styles),
  .data-grid-cell/.cell-1/.cell-0/.cell-pivot (for matrix visualization grid),
  .viz-overlay-stats/.stat-value/.stat-label (for visualization data overlays)
- Remove old CSS keyframe animations (slideIn, flashGreen) that are replaced by
  GSAP-driven animations in visualizations.js

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
